**Overline**

## Working Remotely from Galicia, Spain

Galicia is Spain’s best-kept secret for remote workers. Here’s why northern Spain deserves a spot on your digital nomad radar.

![To develop all these projects, to build a strong community, and to keep with the spirit of a slow traveler lifestyle, we are offering discounts for longer stays. Next year the minimum booking will be one month and we will be opening by March 1st. Looking forward to being part of Anceu's Family.](data:image/png;base64,)

![To develop all these projects, to build a strong community, and to keep with the spirit of a slow traveler lifestyle, we are offering discounts for longer stays. Next year the minimum booking will be one month and we will be opening by March 1st. Looking forward to being part of Anceu's Family.](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Image-diOS-14.jpg)

  

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Fabienne-and-Winchy.jpeg)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/ilsow-coliving.jpg)

### Why Galicia for Remote Work?

Most digital nomads head to Barcelona, Madrid, or the Canary Islands. Galicia offers something different:

-   40-50% lower cost of living than Barcelona or Madrid
-   Excellent internet infrastructure (1Gbps fiber widely available)
-   Mild year-round climate (no extreme heat like southern Spain)
-   Stunning nature without the crowds
-   Authentic Spanish culture (not tourist-oriented)
-   Proximity to Portugal (Porto is 1.5 hours away)

### Cost of Living in Galicia

**Expense**

**Monthly cost**

Coliving (Anceu)

€882 – €1260

Apartment rental (1BR)

€400 – €800

Groceries

€200 – €300

Dining out

€150 – €250

Transport

€50 – €150

Coworking (if needed)

€100 – €200

**TOTAL**

**€1,000 – €1,800**

Compare this to Barcelona (€2,000-3,000/month) or Lisbon (€1,800-2,500/month).

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/10/Anceu-Coliving-Hammock-life-1.jpeg)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/vol1.jpg)

### Internet Quality in Galicia

Galicia has surprisingly excellent internet infrastructure:

-   **Fiber optic coverage**: Available in most towns over 1,000 people
-   **Typical speeds**: 300Mbps – 1Gbps symmetric
-   **At Anceu Coliving**: 1Gbps symmetric fiber
-   **Mobile coverage**: Good 4G/5G in populated areas, patchy in mountains

**Tip**: Always verify internet speed before booking accommodation. We recommend using Speedtest results, not advertised speeds.

## Where to Base Yourself

![City of Pontevedra](data:image/png;base64,)

![City of Pontevedra](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/pontevedra.jpg)

### Pontevedra

**Population**: 83,000  
**Vibe**: Pedestrian-friendly, family-oriented  
**Coworking**: Several options in city center  
**Best for**: Those wanting city amenities with smaller-town feel

![](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAABAAAAAKqAQAAAACMMRGcAAAAAnRSTlMAAHaTzTgAAABrSURBVHja7cEBAQAAAIIg/69uSEABAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAvBtXuQABimusowAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/santiago.jpeg)

### Santiago de Compostela

**Population**: 97,000  
**Vibe**: University town, historic, multiple cafes  
**Coworking**: Multiple spaces  
**Best for**: Culture lovers, social remote workers

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/01/a-coru.jpg)

### A Coruña

**Population**: 255,000  
**Vibe**: Galicia’s largest city, more urban  
**Coworking**: Most options in Galicia  
**Best for**: Those wanting full city services

![Anceu Bird Pic](data:image/png;base64,)

![Anceu Bird Pic](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/bird-pic.jpg)

### Rural Galicia (Villages)

**Population**: Varies (20 – 5,000)  
**Vibe**: Nature immersion, quiet, community-focused  
**Coworking**: Coliving spaces ([Anceu](https://anceu.com/), [Sende](https://sende.co), [iSlow](https://islowcoliving.com/))  
**Best for**: Deep work, nature lovers, community seekers

### Coliving Options in Galicia

**[Anceu Coliving](https://anceu.com/the-space/)** is located in Ponte Caldelas and offers 1Gbps WiFi. Prices range from €882 to €1,260 per month, with stays between 15 and 60 days.

**Sende** is situated near Portugal with fast WiFi, costing approximately €745 per month with flexible stay lengths.

**iSlow** is located in Costa da Morte, offers fast WiFi, variable pricing, and flexible stay options.

![](data:image/png;base64,)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/P1066145.jpg)

![Hike in Anceu with breakers](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAABAAAAAMAAQAAAACSwSigAAAAAnRSTlMAAHaTzTgAAAB2SURBVHja7cEBAQAAAIIg/69uSEABAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAADvBoMPAAHlAN1UAAAAAElFTkSuQmCC)

![Hike in Anceu with breakers](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/Untitled-design-14.jpg)

### Getting Around Galicia

**With a Car (Recommended)**

Having a car is the best way to get around. You can rent one for €300-500 per month from Santiago airport, or buy a reliable used car for €3,000-5,000. Fuel costs €1.50-1.80 per liter, and parking is usually free outside the cities.Without a Car

**If you don’t have a car**, buses connect major cities but run infrequently to villages. Trains operate along the Santiago-Vigo-Pontevedra corridor. Carpooling is common within coliving communities, and BlaBlaCar works well for traveling between cities.Reality check: For rural Galicia (including Anceu), a car significantly improves your experience. Public transport exists but is limited.

### Weather & Best Times to Visit

Season

Temperature

Rain

Notes

Spring (Mar-May)

12-20°C

Moderate

Green landscapes, comfortable

Summer (Jun-Aug)

18-28°C

Low

Warmest, but rarely hot

Autumn (Sep-Nov)

12-20°C

High

Rainy but beautiful

Winter (Dec-Feb)

3-14°C

High

Mild but wet

**Galicia is called *Green Spain*** for a reason—it rains. But it’s rarely extreme, and the landscape is stunning year-round.

![](data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAtkAAAPMAQAAAAC/WpivAAAAAnRSTlMAAHaTzTgAAABuSURBVHja7cExAQAAAMKg9U9tCj+gAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA4GthKwABrQwK/AAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==)

![](https://anceu.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/11/PXL_20240928_105253506.jpg)

### Things to Do (When Not Working)

#### Nature

Galicia offers incredible outdoor experiences. You can hike along the famous Camino de Santiago routes, relax at the beaches of the Rías Baixas, or visit the stunning Cíes Islands (only accessible in summer with a permit required). The Ribeira Sacra wine region is worth exploring, and you’ll find natural hot springs near Ourense.

#### Culture

The region has a rich cultural heritage. Santiago de Compostela’s old town is a UNESCO World Heritage site. Galicia is known for having the best fresh seafood in Spain, along with excellent local wines including Albariño, Godello, and Mencía. Celtic festivals and traditions remain an important part of local culture.

#### From Anceu Specifically

[Staying at Anceu](https://anceu.com/book/) puts you in a great location. Forest hiking trails start right from the door. Beaches are a 30-45 minute drive away, Santiago de Compostela is about an hour, and Porto, Portugal is just 1.5 hours away. Natural hot springs are only a 20 minute drive.

### Visa considerations

EU citizens can live and work in Spain freely. Non-EU remote workers have options:  
**Tourist visa**: Up to 90 days (cannot legally work)  
**Digital Nomad Visa**: Up to 5 years for remote workers  
**Non-lucrative visa**: For those with savings (no work permitted)

## Ready to Try Galicia?

[Join us here](https://anceu.com/book/)

Anceu Coliving offers 15-60 day stays in rural Galicia—perfect for testing whether this region is right for you.

**What’s included?**

Private room with desk

1Gbps fiber WiFi

Multiple coworking spaces

Biweekly communal activities

Weekly community dinners

A whole forest to discover

Village life
